overview: This step applies an XSLT template to input XML documents. It takes raw well-formed XML documents as input, applies the specified XSLT template, and produces a raw document output for the next step in the pipeline. The step supports custom XSLT parameters with variable substitution, and allows configuring custom transformer and XPath factory classes.

xsltPath.description: The full path of the XSLT template to apply on the input documents. Must point to a valid XSLT template file on the filesystem.

paramList.description: A list of parameters to pass to the XSLT template. The format must be name=value with one parameter per line (e.g., 'Lang=en\ntmFile=myTM.tmx'). You can use the following variables in parameter values: ${inputPath} / ${inputPath1} (full path of the first input document), ${inputURI} / ${inputURI1} (URI of the first input document), ${outputPath} / ${outputPath1} (full path of the first output document), ${srcLang} (language part of the source locale, e.g. 'de' for 'de-ch'), ${trgLang} (language part of the target locale, e.g. 'ja' for 'ja-jp'), ${inputPath2} / ${inputURI2} (full path/URI of the second input document), ${inputPath3} / ${inputURI3} (full path/URI of the third input document).

useCustomTransformer.description: When enabled, uses a custom XSLT transformer implementation and optionally a custom XPath engine, instead of the default ones provided by the current Java settings. The specified factory classes must be available on the Java classpath. The default transformer and XPath classes depend on the implementation and version of your Java Virtual Machine (see TransformerFactory.newInstance() documentation for lookup details).

factoryClass.description: The fully-qualified class name of the custom XSLT TransformerFactory implementation to use. For example: 'net.sf.saxon.TransformerFactoryImpl'. The class must be available on your Java classpath.

xpathClass.description: The fully-qualified class name of the custom XPath factory implementation to use. For example: 'net.sf.saxon.xpath.XPathFactoryImpl'. This is optional even when useCustomTransformer is enabled. The class must be available on your Java classpath.

passOnOutput.description: When true, the output of the XSLT transformation is passed as the raw document to the next step in the pipeline.

processingNotes: Input documents must be well-formed XML documents.

The default XSLT transformer and XPath classes depend on the implementation and version of the Java Virtual Machine. See the TransformerFactory.newInstance() documentation for details on how the class lookup is performed.
Variable substitution in parameters supports references to up to three input documents and one output document, as well as source and target language codes (language part only, not full locale).

examples.title: XSLT parameters with variables
examples.description: Passing parameters to an XSLT template using the name=value format, one per line. Demonstrates use of the ${inputPath2} variable to reference the second input document.

examples.title: Custom Saxon transformer configuration
examples.description: Configuring the step to use Saxon as the XSLT transformer and XPath engine instead of the JVM default. Requires useCustomTransformer to be enabled and the Saxon classes to be on the classpath.
examples.input:
useCustomTransformer: true
factoryClass: net.sf.saxon.TransformerFactoryImpl
xpathClass: net.sf.saxon.xpath.XPathFactoryImpl
